methodsAttempted.query: site:le.ac.uk ("generative AI" OR "ChatGPT" OR "artificial intelligence") (policy OR guidance OR assessment OR students OR staff) "University of Leicester"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official University of Leicester PDF Policy on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning, Teaching and Assessment and the Academic quality and standards policy index.
The public policy index states that further staff guidance is available on SharePoint and requires login.
Robots.txt allowed the public policy paths for the default crawler; HTTP HEAD checks returned 200 for the policy index and PDF.

artifacts.summary: Official-domain discovery found a public University of Leicester PDF policy governing generative AI in learning, teaching, and assessment, plus the official academic quality policy index that links to it. The index also identifies staff guidance on SharePoint, but that source was not crawled because it requires login.

targets.sourceTitle: Policy on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning, Teaching and Assessment

stopConditions: Stop after official public University of Leicester sources support narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer private SharePoint staff guidance content from the public index note.
Do not broaden the learning, teaching, and assessment policy to staff research, which the policy excludes.

artifacts.claimText: For assessment, the University of Leicester uses three categories for generative AI use: red where AI must not be used, amber where AI may support assessment but not generate submitted content, and green where AI may generate assessment content within the brief.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-backed candidate; not canonical and not human-reviewed.
Approved because the claim follows the policy's stated red, amber, and green assessment categories without expanding beyond assessment use.
Approved with 'should' wording to match the policy language.
Approved because the policy uses mandatory language for use involving personal data without explicit consent.
Approved as a narrow claim about assessment marking and feedback review requirements.

artifacts.title: University of Leicester AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Official public sources for the University of Leicester include a Summer 2025 Policy on Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning, Teaching and Assessment. The staged claims cover the university-level framework, red/amber/green assessment categories, student academic-integrity training on AI, personal-data restrictions, and AI-supported marking or feedback review requirements. Staff-only SharePoint guidance was identified but not crawled because the public index marks it as login required.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staff SharePoint guidance linked from the public policy index was not crawled because it requires login.
The policy states that staff research is governed separately by the Research Code of Conduct, so these staged claims are limited to learning, teaching, and assessment.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
